reasoning: For 'comes out the front door', I used 'taxtiyi' (come out/exit verb) with no location prefix (as the front door had none in sentence 1), followed by 'n chiki wa:qni'' (the house + fox).
For 'goes into the chimney', I used 'Akpu:' prefix (associated with chimney/roof in sentences 2-3) + 'tanu:yi' (go into verb) + 'n chiki wa:qni''.
For 'comes out a hole in the roof', I used 'Akpu:' prefix (for roof) + 'tukuta' (come out verb) + 'n chiki wa:qni''.
For 'comes in the back door', I used 'Cha:qe:' prefix (back door from sentence 4) + 'tanu:yi' (go into/enter verb) + 'n chiki wa:qni''.
For 'goes into a window on the side of the house', I used 'Lakpa:' prefix (side location from sentence 5) + 'tanu:yi' (go into verb) + 'n chiki wa:qni''.
For 'comes out of a flower box on the back of the house', I used 'Cha:qe:' prefix (back location) + 'tukuta' (come out verb) + 'n chiki wa:qni''.
